title: Egoist Tutorial Nr.2 Slicer
transcript: okay in this video I'm going to start demonstrating the slicer window of egoist and explaining what you can do here so let's check out this Loop we have you can hear that it's been sliced up and rearranged but if I click on this play button here we can hear the original sample so it's so different when you slice it up and start rearranging it can create music that you never even imagined from this sample cool okay so that's the first thing to know is that you can click on this play button here and the second thing to know is that if you click on any of the areas here the slices you can listen to what they sound like so that's really cool we can also change the pitch so I'm just going to leave that at zero for now and you can also change change the position of these slices really really easily okay next up I want to show you that you can click on the random button here and that's going to randomize the slic okay so there's the randomized button there you can also adjust the sensitivity which seems to adjust the actual length of the slices the uh amount of time they take to play each slice just going to come over to the uh pattern tab here next up you can change the actual length of each individual sample so let's see what that sounds like so you can hear each slice is very very short in length now let's bring it back to 100% And then we can adjust the Decay envelope the release envelope and the sustain length it's kind of like a very basic adsr envelope there for amplitude and then the max out you can see graphically how that changes as we move it what that does is it adjusts the overall level of the sound so so about there sounds good I think just keep adjusting to taste and then down here we have a slices section a pitch section and then an attack section where you can change the attack of each individual slice the attack time a Decay section and then a level section so you get a lot of control over the kind of attack Decay sustain and release when you include this release section up here so let's just see what we can do there and I'm going to start adjusting some of these slices now and seeing what we can do so you can activate and deactivate a slice we can choose whether we want the slice to play forwards or backwards this direction button and now we can nudge everything over to the left just a very quick way to easily change up the Rhythm and the melody move everything up one so while I love about these left right up and down arrows is that you can change up the melody in ways that you would never even imagine of doing if you were just using a conventional digital audio workstations piano roll so this is a really cool thing now another really cool thing is that you can click on copy here then we can click onto a different pattern and click on paste and now we have the same Melody let's change it up a bit let's make this one go down and to the right so it sounds like this but when I switch back to pattern one it will hear the original so let's keep working on pattern one we can change the pitch of each individual slice we can make it go up by an octave or down by an octave we can change the attack to be slower we can make the Decay faster slower so you get a lot of control over this and change the levels of course and it's just really really cool again you can nudge over what we've just done with the pitch you can nudge that over to the left or the right you can nudge nudge everything up one or down one in terms of pitch and again with the attack and Decay shapes that we've drawn in here we can nudge those over to the left and the right so you really get a lot of control if you want you can play around with it randomly or you can really dial in exactly what you want depending on how you like to make music so it really opens up your mind to new creativity and new ways of thinking so thanks for watching this demonstration on the slicer window in the next video we're going to take a look at the Basse and beat and I'll show you just how much control you can get over these sounds as well so speak to you soon
